abstract: The integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in service provision has prompted a revaluation of how we design service encounters, given the emergent role this technology plays in services. This article introduces the concept of the Hybrid Service Encounter to explore the evolving interplay between humans and AI in service contexts. We propose a 2 × 2 framework that categorizes service interactions into four distinct quadrants based on whether the provider and user are human or AI. Drawing on service design literature and current developments in AI, we analyze the implications of these hybrid encounters for service design roles, processes, and outputs. The article further identifies key areas for future research to guide the development of human-centered and ethical AI-powered services. Our contribution extends service design theory and practice by offering a framework to guide the integration of AI in service encounters.

affiliation: Design Department, Politecnico di Milano, Milano, Italy
authors_full.name: Marzia Mortati

affiliation: Design Department, Politecnico di Milano, Milano, Italy
authors_full.name: Giovanna Viana Mundstock Freitas

ur.ai_reason: 核心议题即AI服务设计，含用户-AI互动框架
ur.tldr: 针对AI在服务提供中的整合趋势，重新审视服务接触设计方式，提出“混合服务遭遇”（Hybrid Service Encounter）概念，构建以提供方（人类/AI）与用户（人类/AI）为轴的2×2分类框架，分析混合遭遇对服务设计角色、流程与产出的影响，并识别以人为中心且合乎伦理的AI驱动服务未来研究方向。

title: AI in Service Design: A New Framework for Hybrid Human–AI Service Encounters
title_zh: 服务设计中的人工智能：人机混合服务交互的新框架
